results.justification: EXACT TWIN — FourierAttention projects head dimensions onto orthogonal Fourier bases + fixed-length spectral coefficients IS the per-channel harmonic-basis projection mechanism.
EXACT TWIN — FourierCompress retains dominant low-frequency coefficients IS the sparse top-K coefficient compression.
EXACT TWIN — SpecQuant channel-wise spectral truncation in Fourier domain IS the per-channel sparse-coefficient allocation.
Harmonizer harmonic tokenization is the single-channel harmonic-basis projection paradigm applied to multimodal signals.
Spectral Modulation on Transformer weights IS the channel-level spectral-allocation effect at weight matrix granularity.
Fine-grained MoE quantization at expert-channel level covers per-channel spectral resource allocation paradigm.
Graph-spectral decomposition for parameter coordination is single-channel/per-component spectral allocation at parameter granularity.
EXACT TWIN — LESTD learning-based spectral truncation IS the learned-top-K coefficient selection mechanism.
